title: derris emarginata
intro: Derris emarginata is a plant species in the legume family, Fabaceae, native to tropical Africa and parts of Asia, known for its medicinal and insecticidal properties. The plant contains rotenone, a toxic compound with insecticidal and antimicrobial properties, which is used to treat various health conditions. Derris emarginata has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation and killing bacteria and parasites. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its extracts have been used in traditional medicine for centuries.
